REQUIREMENT: execution_status.csv is the sole authoritative execution outcome; logs, file existence, and stdout are not contract signals.
execution_status.csv contains exactly five columns in order: EXECUTION_STATUS, INPUT_FOUND, ERROR_MESSAGE, N_T, MAIN_RESULT_SUMMARY.
EXECUTION_STATUS may be only PARTIAL, SUCCESS, or FAILED.
A completed run ends with exactly one final row: SUCCESS or FAILED. PARTIAL is for checkpoints only and must not be the final state.
PARTIAL rows may be written multiple times; each write overwrites the file (no append).
Final SUCCESS or FAILED is written once per call using a temp file and atomic replace; it must be the last signaling write for that run.
On failure, EXECUTION_STATUS=FAILED and ERROR_MESSAGE is non-empty.
On success, EXECUTION_STATUS=SUCCESS and ERROR_MESSAGE is empty.
Writer rejects empty EXECUTION_STATUS, invalid enum, SUCCESS with non-empty error text, and PARTIAL as a final write.
All writes to execution_status.csv in the Switching module go through Switching/utils/writeSwitchingExecutionStatus.m only.
